microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory food THEN 2 min BECAUSE they generally like food served warm without extra delay. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND fatigued AND refrigerated savory food THEN 3 min BECAUSE hotter food is preferred when they want comfort and fewer interruptions. IF in a hurry AND any context AND refrigerated savory food THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed matters more than maximum warmth. IF any context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting with another person nearby AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory food THEN 2 min BECAUSE moderate reheating balances comfort and social pacing. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet AND distracted AND refrigerated savory food THEN 3 min BECAUSE they may eat more slowly and want the food to stay warm longer.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no competing visual target to protect. IF watching television directly in front AND any affect AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE preserving the screen view matters more than shortest motion. IF working on a laptop or tablet directly in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep the workspace visible. IF social partner is seated on the left AND any affect AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE they want to maintain visual access to that person. IF social partner is seated on the right AND any affect AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE they want to maintain visual access to that person. IF social setting or screen-viewing context AND fatigued AND any food THEN the relevant left/front/right minimize option still applies BECAUSE this user prioritizes reducing distraction over speed unless fatigue creates physical effort issues, which occlusion does not.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE they want efficient but not abrupt motion. IF any context AND fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE comfort and predictability outweigh speed when energy is low. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE they tolerate brisker motion to shorten meal time. IF social setting with another person nearby AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE moderate speed feels socially acceptable without being too slow. IF social setting with another person nearby AND fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue wins over social efficiency in this user's tie-break policy. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE they prefer steady motion that is not too distracting.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND firm or slippery foods like meat or fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE acquisition reliability is the main priority. IF any context AND neutral AND soft elongated foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites can be cleaner and easier to manage. IF social setting with another person nearby AND any affect except fatigue AND messy or failure-prone foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding failed acquisitions is socially preferable. IF any context AND fatigued AND elongated foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE smaller narrower bites feel easier when tired. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any skewerable food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE higher acquisition success reduces retries. IF any context AND dessert or brownie-like items that hold together well THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE stable pickup is preferred over bite narrowness.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE they prefer streamlined interaction and can tolerate occasional failed bite transfer attempts. IF social setting with another person nearby AND neutral AND failure-prone foods like slippery fruit or meat THEN yes BECAUSE they want to avoid awkward empty-fork transfers in front of others. IF any context AND fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE minimizing effort dominates even if reliability drops slightly. IF working on a laptop/tablet or watching television AND distracted AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE attention is divided and explicit confirmation helps catch acquisition failures. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any food THEN no BECAUSE reducing interaction time is more important than extra checking. IF social setting with another person nearby AND fatigued AND failure-prone foods THEN no BECAUSE this user consistently breaks ties in favor of lower effort under fatigue.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE they cannot lean forward and need direct delivery. IF any context AND fatigued AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE fatigue further reduces tolerance for self-initiated reaching. IF social setting with another person nearby AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical limitations make outside-mouth transfer impractical despite possible social preference for distance. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most reliable and efficient given their trunk limitations. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet AND distracted AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE direct placement reduces the need for precise timing from the user. IF any context AND any affect AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is incompatible with their inability to lean forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the default transfer mode is inside mouth transfer. IF social setting with another person nearby AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer remains unsuitable for their body mechanics. IF any context AND fatigued AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue only strengthens the need for inside-mouth transfer. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE they still need direct delivery rather than a reachable stop point. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet AND distracted AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE transfer mode does not switch to outside-mouth. IF social setting with another person nearby AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE they want a quieter interaction that is less disruptive to conversation. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE they prioritize efficiency when nothing important is being visually blocked. IF any context AND in a hurry AND any task THEN 10 sec BECAUSE they prefer rapid progression. IF any context AND fatigued AND any task THEN 10 sec BECAUSE they want the robot to take over quickly with minimal effort from them. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet AND distracted AND any task THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE they want ample time to notice and intervene while attention is elsewhere.
